definition: A being, what has come to be, or an existent thing, depending on whether the context points to persons or to conditioned becoming.
notes: Keep being as the default when the text names beings or a concrete class of what is encountered, as in MN 1. Use what has come to be when the conditioned, processive force of the family is foregrounded, especially near bhava or yathabhuta language. Do not turn the term into a metaphysical substance or collapse it into later element-system talk.
